page: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 architecture server within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, meaning physical CPU resources are shared with other tenants despite the high core count.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in several areas. The server achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth, with read speeds of 414.9 GB/sec and write speeds of 213.09 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 88688.86 and a PassMark CPU score of 82776.54. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top tier.

Given the high vCPU-to-RAM ratio and large local NVMe storage, this server is configured for memory-intensive workloads and database operations. PassMark database operations scored in the top 25%, and Redis SET operations reached 85.37 million operations per second. It also supports moderate LLM inference, processing 135M parameter models at 294.26 tokens/sec for text generation. The shared CPU allocation offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for resource-dense workloads that require substantial memory and storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated 100% CPU utilization.
description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 server in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 48 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs at 2.0 GHz, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 2880 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating with a shared CPU allocation, it provides a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core CPU execution and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. Database operations and Redis SET throughput are highly competitive, ranking in the top 25%. This configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and moderate language model inference where high memory capacity and local NVMe storage are required, while the shared CPU model offers a cost-efficient alternative to dedicated hardware.
og_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s is an x86_64 server with 48 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe SSD. It features a shared CPU allocation and delivers top-tier memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-m-48c-384g-2880s features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 2880 GB NVMe SSD, offering high memory density with shared CPU allocation.
tagline: An x86_64 server combining 384 GB of RAM, 2880 GB NVMe storage, and 48 shared AMD EPYC cores for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model.
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.
2880 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier large-block memory bandwidth and single-core CPU performance.
Strong database performance, scoring in the top 25% for Redis and PassMark database tests.
Suited for memory-heavy applications, database hosting, and moderate LLM inference.
